listing_name: Pearl Mill Flats ( formerly Duke Tower ) unit 141
description:
An Old Historic Complex with a rebuilt pool and a huge courtyard and garden with games and a giant chessboard. a cozy 1 bedroom apartment in downtown Durham walking from Main Street.

The space
1 king bedroom unit in the former Duke Tower condotel complex , now Pearl Mill Flats condos in downtown Durham with large courtyard and garden that features giant chess game & shuffleboard !
Access to a large pool.
All utilities included with Wifi and Internet access. Coin operated laundryroom is at one end of complex . Just go right when you leave unit all the way past pool till end of walkway then turn right and it is the before last door on your left, only accessible with your fob on keychain.
The trash is in front of complex, to the extreme left and right on the Trinity Ave. side. The Recycling is only on the extreme right of complex on the Trinity Ave. front.
Absolutely no smoking in unit or courtyard. There is a designated smoker's shed in parking lot behind complex.

listing_name: The Cat House Guest Suite
description:
1930's bungalow located conveniently close to Duke and downtown Durham. There are plenty of really good dining places within a 3 minute drive from the house. Grocery store walking distance and a good neighborhood for walking and running. Study, work and rest peacefully in a quiet house with plenty of space to claim for your own.

The space
Charming bungalow close to downtown Durham, Duke University and historic Durham. Great restaurants a hop, skip and a jump from the house. The Nasher Art Museum, Durham Bulls ballpark, Sarah P Duke Gardens, and so much more are within 2 miles of this fun, colorful home lived in by an artist.

The Cat House Guest Suite is named for the big yellow cat hanging on the front of the house. And inside there is my cat, Sash, and an outdoor, my cat, Fiona. f you are allergic to cats, you will not be happy here.

The suite is designed with Asian art I have collected. There is a sleeping area with a queen-sized bed and a sitting area with a table and futon couch (with a memory foam topper.) This large room can accommodate a couple and up to 2 children. You have your own toilet and shower/tub area with a sink in an adjoining area.

Guests have dining and living room, deck, kitchen and laundry room privileges. I have my own suite area; this house is big enough for guests to relax in privacy .

I enjoy sharing the grittiness of Durham with visitors. I've been here 29 years and know my way around to wherever you might want to go. This is the perfect place to call home when you are visiting the town that tobacco built.

Guest access
You are welcome to use the kitchen as you would your own. I provide oils, spices and vinegars.

There's a large cupboard in the kitchen for guests and refrigerator space as you claim.

There's recycling and composting.

I make coffee every morning, but there are also all kinds of teas available for guests to choose from.

During your stay
I am retired and spend much of the time at home, but because of the way the house is configured, I can be in my suite and the guest won't even know I'm home.

I respect your privacy and am also delighted to interact as best suits my and your availability and needs. My cat, Sash, is friendly and is available to guests. So even when I'm not at home, guests are welcome to enjoy Sash.

Other things to note
I have an indoor cat named, Sash, and an outdoor feral cat named, Fiona.

listing_name: Beautiful Room near Duke, RTP, UNC
description:
Everyone seems to love my home’s location. It’s 12 minutes away from the RTP and close to Duke University and UNC at Chapel Hill.

The space
Rent a room in my traditional style, 2,000 sq. ft house near south Durham. Owner travels and would like to find a responsible person to rent a room with a private bathroom. The available room has a large Queen-size bed, ceiling fans, desk and chair, and fine art. House is located in a quiet residential neighborhood near South Point Mall, RTP, Duke University and UNC in Chapel Hill. There are restaurant and shops five minutes away. The room rental includes high speed internet, cable TV and utilities. The kitchen has been updated with all new appliances and is very large. There's a laundry room, library, and two dinning areas.
The back yard has a deck and a treed yard. The house has a large rocking chair porch and is beautifully landscaped.

Guest access
Enter through the front door or backyard deck

During your stay
Usually, I am not around. If in town, I am busy, but always friendly.

listing_name: Contactless cozy BD/Bath just north of downtown
description:
COVID-19: Lysol is used on surfaces and sanitize setting on washer/dryer. Key lock box used. Close to Downtown/Duke/RTP, your cozy private bedroom and bathroom are conveniently located just 5.6 miles north of Main St in a peaceful neighborhood. We are flexible on arrival and departure times. Two guests can have both bedrooms for $5 more. We are 6 miles or 15 minutes from Duke's East campus, and we're 20 minutes from Duke Chapel. UNC Chapel Hill's 30 minutes south. We have 1 foster cat downstairs

The space
The listing is for one bedroom, with the option of adding the other bedroom. The rate showing is for one bedroom, but you can request both bedrooms for an extra $10 nightly if only 2 guests total. If there are 3-4 guests, then the rate automatically updates and includes both bedrooms. The loft has a minifridge, microwave, and a single cup coffee maker that can brew either pods or ground coffee. We're about five miles north of Main St.

Guest access
we're not offering the shared spaces (kitchen and laundry). You have private spaces of the bedroom/s, private bath upstairs and the sitting area in the loft which has a mini fridge, coffee maker, and microwave, plus the piano room with Roku TV. If you do the twin bedroom, that space has a TV with Roku too. Parking is in our driveway.

During your stay
We love Durham and are available for any local questions/directions/suggestions. There are great places for takeout meals and some outdoor seating.

Other things to note
We have 1 foster cat as Nov 2025 that we keep in our space. The whole home is on a filtered water system. Please do not put wet towels on wood surfaces, and at check-out put used towels on the bathroom counter. Smoking is not allowed in any way inside.

listing_name: Popular 3BR Suite, SWDurham, Chapel Hill, UNC
description:
COUNTRY IN THE CITY.
PRIVATE 3-Bdrm apartment with GOURMET KITCHEN. SW Durham only a few minutes to UNC, Whole Foods or I40. Pond, trees, gravel road, horses feels like country living. Pets allowed. ONLY 2 people living long term but may have guests.
This private apartment upstairs in duplex house has 2 bedrooms plus 3rd bedroom furnished as an office with futon bed.
Another apartment downstairs in the same house is where I live with a dog and cat, so you will hear & see us at times.

The space
This rental has been very popular on Airbnb, with great reviews. Spacious and bright 1400 sq ft separate private apartment upstairs in house. There is another separate apartment downstairs, where I live. It is completely nicely furnished with linens and all utilities, TV, strong wifi. Terrific well-appointed Kitchen with Gas Range, Professional Hood Vent, Dishwasher, RO filtered water. Stocked with enough basic supplies for your first morning or two: coffee, toilet paper, etc.
Nice open living space and kitchen on one level, then up a half flight of steps to top level with 3 rooms (bedrooms or office space), and 1 generous bathroom. There are comfortable Queen beds in both the Master & Guest room . A double Futon Sofa Bed is in the office/3rd Bedroom . The sofa in the living room does not fold out but is long and comfortable for 1 extra person for a short stay.
I am happy to offer early check-in OR late check-out if it is possible; just ask. Pets with permission, please. Contact me for Long-term pricing options.
This listing is best for only 2 guests long-term due to the septic system, but can accommodate up to 5 people for short stays. Please book the correct number of guests; or if you rent long-term, let me know if you expect short-term guests.
Plenty of outdoor space. Unique location on GRAVEL ROAD that feels like the country but is CLOSE TO EVERYTHING and literally only a few minutes away from Meadowmont, FRIDAY CENTER, UNC, and I40 (to Southpoint, Durham, RTP). Woods and a pond create a feeling of natural solitude; but note that the gravel road can have potholes and be dusty at times if you are very protective of your car.
House is divided into 2 separate private living spaces, so you will see me and my dog and cat on the property; as well as Genaro , my employee for 16+ years.
Your living area has a wool-blend carpet but all other areas of house are hard surfaces with rugs. This is a great home for a traveling professional, a couple, parents with older children. The pond could be a risk for very young children.
Shared Laundry room available for use at your convenience.
It is best to have a car for this location. Outside steps have no handrails, so not best for individuals with limited mobility .
\Country living\" has pros and cons. I have heard raves from my guests about the loveliness of the spot; but there are plenty of bugs in the warm months; there are various critters around in the woods

listing_name: Cozy Cabin Style Bungalow House Durham NC
description:
With a car my cabin-like bungalow is 3-5 min to downtown Durham, 15 min RTP & 10min to Duke. Kitchen, Dining Room, Living Room, Bedroom, Bathroom. Sleeps two guests. Washer/Dryer. The back yard features a community-like feeling with several Vintage Campers as Cottages hosting air bnb guests such as grad students, nurses, musicians, teachers. This is the main house of the two acre property. Not for fussy people. Farm house from 1937. The floors are wavy, and parts of the house have settled.

The space
With a car my cabin-like bungalow is 3-5 min to downtown Durham, 15 min RTP & 10min to Duke. Kitchen, Dining Room, Living Room, Bedroom, Bathroom. Sleeps two guests. Washer/Dryer. The back yard features a community-like feeling with several Vintage Camper as Cottages hosting air bnb guests such as grad students, nurses, musicians, teachers. This is the main house of the two acre property.

Guest access
Guests have access to all but one room of the home.

Other things to note
My house is from 1937 and not perfect. If you are looking for the Hilton, this is not for you. It's tidy, quaint and outside of the downtown area. It is zoned rural residential, meaning it's sort of in the country but with several other houses and neighborhoods near by. My neighbors are good ol’ southern folk who have generations of roots on our land. It is unlikely that any interaction with them will take place but if so, they are very nice. Any more questions, please feel free to contact me.

listing_name: Private Bath, Near Duke | Clean Dogcentric Home
description:
🏳️‍🌈 Sunlit room + private guest bath Hosted by 2 humans + 2 dogs*.

Clean, cozy, dog-loving home with professional cleaning + weekly fresh linens. Walk to Duke, cafes, Whole Foods, & Cocoa Cinnamon.

Sleep like a log on the luxury Saatva mattress.

*Guests must love (not just tolerate) dogs! Ours are professionally bathed every 4 wks for allergies (theirs + ours). This is a “dogs on furniture” household, but we keep things very clean; you can decline letting them in the room

The space
Nestled in the heart of one of Durham’s most beloved neighborhoods, our home blends comfort, style, and convenience — ideal for medical rotations, short-term contracts, or relocation. Located near Oval Park, Broad Street, and Ninth Street, you’ll be perfectly positioned to enjoy some of the best parts of Durham on bike or foot.

This is a clean, dog-loving 1927 bungalow where historic charm meets modern updates. The guest room is tucked off the main hall with a newly remodeled private bath for exclusive use. You’ll share the well-appointed kitchen, breezy porches, newly remodeled laundry room, and fenced 1/2 acre piedmont prairie with your hosts!

We wash guest linens weekly and have a biweekly professional housekeeper, so we mean it when we say this is a clean, serene space!

Old West Durham is known for its artsy, down-to-earth atmosphere and scenic streets. Take a leisurely stroll to Oval Park, the best coffee shop in town (Cocoa Cinnamon), or local fave popsicle-and-gourmet-groceries spot, Locopops. Enjoy the lush tree-filled streets and gardens in our neighborhood, or wander down to Ninth and Broad Streets for shopping, groceries (Harris Teeter and Whold Foods), and restaurants.

Convenient Yet Quiet Location: While our neighborhood is a vibrant community of its own, our street is peaceful and quiet enough to support full unwinding and relaxation after a day of exploring. Our proximity to Duke University offers guests opportunities to engage in all the campus has to offer. We are equidistant to both east and west campuses and walking distance from the main hospital.

Dog-Friendly Atmosphere: As dog lovers, we welcome guests who want to bring one furry, dog-friendly dog along. Our two dogs, both Great Pyrenees mixes, are certainly not model canine citizens (one is a little anxious and barks when she meets new people at home) but they are great snuggle buddies and do their very best to welcome guests!

Whether you’re visiting Durham for business, pleasure, or a bit of both, our home is a convenient, welcoming base. Come stay with us and experience the warmth and charm of Old West Durham – we look forward to hosting you!

Parking: Easy on-street parking. We appreciate it when guests park directly in front of our house if possible.

About Us: We are two creative individuals who cherish our community and enjoy meeting new people. We are accommodating to the needs of both introverts and extroverts. So you’ll be equally welcome to hang out with us or keep to yourself. Either way, we're excited to share our home and help you experience the best of Durham.

Our proximity to Duke University offers guests opportunities to engage in all the campus has to offer. We are equidistant to both east and west campuses and an easy walking or biking distance (0.7 mile) from the main hospital. If you’ll be deeper on west campus than the hospital and are carrying a lot of books, biking or ride sharing is the best option, followed by taking the Duke buses. Please message us if you’re visiting for Duke-related reasons and are unsure about how walkable your specific campus location will be from the house.

Guest access
Home Amenities:
* Living Spaces: Guests have full access to our kitchen, dining nook, and living room with fireplace and Roku TV.
* Outdoor Areas: Relax in our vibrant garden, which includes a deck and tall privacy fence, or people watch on our elevated front patio. Perfect for unwinding after a day of exploration.

Wifi, laundry, kitchen, street parking, private adjacent bathroom, backyard with deck and tall privacy fence, elevated front porch, fenced in front yard.

During your stay
We keep busy with work and friends. Guests who are independent, self-directed or looking for more interaction are all welcome. We love to meet you, chat if we're home, and are available for questions and needs at the house and via phone.

Other things to note
Please do not book with us if you do not genuinely see sharing the space with our dogs as a perk!

Our dogs are both Pyr mixes. They are very independent and stubborn and like to snuggle sometimes, but aren’t big lickers, or jumpers. One mostly keeps to himself with rare exceptions and the other loves people but can take some time to regulate her anxiety (i.e., nervous barking) when less familiar people enter the house. She does best with Dog People but we have a system in place if she has trouble getting used to a particular guest.

There is also a table, chairs, ottoman, treadmill and yoga mat in the room for relaxing. A microwave, refrigerator and coffee maker are provided for your convenience. Feel free to enjoy your meals at the counter, dining room table or in your room. All common spaces are available as a courtesy including the living room, screened porch, kitchen and dining area and are not subject to specific Airbnb guidelines.

listing_name: Studio with Deck, Walk to Duke
description:
Enjoy a private studio apartment in Durham's Historic Walltown, just 1.5 blocks from Duke's East Campus. This peaceful retreat, part of a historic craftsman home, features a private entrance, a spacious deck, a fully equipped kitchen, and a dedicated workspace. With a Queen bed and a fold-out couch, it comfortably accommodates up to 3 guests. Enjoy modern comforts in a charming, character-filled setting.

The space
Discover your private retreat nestled at the back of a beautiful historic craftsman home. This studio-style apartment is filled with character, from the stunning golden heart pine floors to the charming antique hardware. The space is thoughtfully arranged for comfort and convenience.

Layout & Features:
-Spacious eat-in kitchen with a gas range and updated countertops.
-Main studio room with a comfortable Queen bed and a separate fold-out couch for lounging.
-Dedicated workspace perfect for remote work or study.
-Large windows providing abundant natural light.
-In-unit washer and dryer located in a convenient nook.
-Newly renovated bathroom with a luxurious skylight.
-Modern comforts including Wi-Fi, air conditioning, and heating.

Entertainment:
-Smart TV with access to your personal Netflix, Amazon, or Hulu accounts.
-YouTubeTV is provided for live and on-demand channels.

Stocked for Your Stay:
-Coffee Bar: High-quality coffee, creamer, sugar, drip coffee maker, and a water boiler.
-Kitchen Essentials: Filtered water, EV olive oil, Himalayan salt & pepper grinders, cookware, serving ware, toaster, blender, and a hand mixer.
-Bath & Laundry: Quality shampoo, conditioner, body wash, a hair dryer, and a starter supply of 'free & clear' laundry detergent. All linens and cleaning supplies are unscented, ideal for guests with allergies or sensitivities.

Guest access
Guests will have exclusive and private access to the entire back apartment unit and the attached private deck.

-Private Entrance: Follow the gravel path from the street to your own entrance at the back of the house.
-Self Check-In: Enjoy the convenience of keyless entry with an electronic keypad, allowing for flexible arrival times.
-Outdoor Space: The spacious deck, complete with a bistro table and chairs overlooking the verdant backyard, is yours to enjoy.

Other things to note
Please be aware of the following details to ensure a smooth and pleasant stay:

-This private apartment is the back unit of a larger historic home. The front portion of the house is a separate rental property.

Built in the 1920s, this 1,500sq/ft house is brimming with historic charm but is modern where you need it: a beautifully remodeled kitchen and bathroom as well as original glass doorknobs and refinished hardwoods floors throughout, a wood burning fireplace in the living room, and 10-foot ceilings and large windows offer lots of light. listing_name: Beautiful Studio in Heart of Downtown
description:
Located in the heart of downtown Durham and all its great culture beats! Just steps away from Durham Performing Arts Center, the 21c Museum Hotel, Carolina Theater, American Tobacco, Durham Bulls Stadium, and the popular restaurants that Downtown is known for.

The space
Located in the heart of downtown Durham, this light-filled studio features a high ceiling, large windows, two skylights, hardwood floors, original art and an open living plan. There is a dishwasher, washer/dryer and an intercom service connected to the front and back door. A full-size bed within a loft in the apartment and an excellent queen-size sofa bed in the common area (the mattress is very comfortable). The apartment is on the second floor with a staircase of 27 steps leading from the ground floor.

Guest access
Entire apartment.

Other things to note
The full- sized bed pictured in the ad is a loft within the apartment. The built in ladder, pictured by the bathroom, leads to that loft. There is a large rice paper shade over the opening of the loft space.

The second bed is a new La-z-boy queen sofa bed. I upgraded the mattress and it truly is the most comfortable sofa bed mattress I've ever slept on.

The loft is quiet. The walls in the building are well insulated so I rarely hear my neighbors and you rarely hear loud noises from the outside at night. During the day, you'll hear a low traffic noise, however it's minimal.

A dog occasionally visits, however he is never on any of the furniture and the floor and rugs are cleaned after his visits.

Parking: There is a lot behind the building, paid parking of $2.25 an hour from 8 AM to 7 PM, free evenings and weekends. There is free two hour parking on Ramseur Street behind the building and metered parking on the other surrounding streets.

There are two entrances to the lot behind the building. The main one is on Corcoran Street between West Main and Ramseur St. The other is on Ramseur St, between Corcoran and Chapel Hill St. The building is in the center of the open lot. If you enter through the Corcoran entrance, to get closer to the building, you go through the tiered area, down the ramp and aim for the middle of the lot.

Smoking or vaping of any substances is not permitted on the premises.

There are great outdoor spaces, comfy beds, and a charming, unique building. The 1st-floor condo is next to the pool, gardens, cookout areas, shuffleboard, and large chessboard. Close to Duke East Campus and Duke Hospital. Well-suited to couples, solo travelers, and business travelers. Small dog <25 pounds allowed for $87/stay. Pool opens 5/2/26.

The space
The condo is very clean and it goes through a disinfecting process and has its own HVAC system. Everything available that is needed for a comfortable stay. Keurig coffee and still and carbonated water are provided. Pearl Mill Flats is within easy walking distance of downtown Durham restaurants and entertainment venues (15 minute walk). My favorite restaurant, Blue Note Grill (great food and live music) is less than two blocks away. Durham Performing Arts Center and the Durham Bulls Baseball Park are about a 2-minute drive or 25-minute walk from Duke Tower. Dog fee is $87/two. Max of 2 dogs under 25lbs allowed. Sorry no cats allowed! Pool is *opening 5/2/26*

Guest access
All areas of the property are accessible to our guests. These areas include lovely gardens in the courtyard, grill and picnic areas, seating and conversation areas, shuffleboard, human-sized chess, miniature golf area, and a large lighted pool *Opening 5/2/26*. Parking is ample and very close to your unit. The property is protected with security cameras and ample lighting.

Other things to note
There is a very comfortable queen sofa bed with an upgraded foam mattress. Also a very comfortable recliner. The pool, decking and landscaping around it were renovated in the summer of 2017. Max of two dogs. One dog is $50/stay and two dogs are $87 total/stay. They must be house broken, up to date on vaccines and flea control and well behaved (minimal barking). Pool hours are dawn to dusk.

listing_name: Perfectly Appointed @ Duke-Blue Devil Living!
description:
Experience Duke at its best from our centrally located duplex between East & West Campus. With the campus bus stop right outside, easily explore Duke, Durham's museums, and local restaurants. Enjoy the perfect blend of comfort and style, featuring Little Waves coffee, a retro bathroom with eco-friendly products, and hotel-quality bedrooms. Duke Hospital is just minutes away, making our home ideal for your stay. Live like a Blue Devil at the heart of Duke and Durham. Book now.

The space
This charming 1940s duplex, located in the historic Duke University neighborhood, offers the perfect blend of vintage charm and modern comfort. Recently updated, the two-bedroom home boasts an updated interior while preserving its historic appeal.

Bathroom:
The bathroom boasts a luxurious highlight - a spacious rain head shower with an exposed pipe design, controlled by a thermostatic mixing valve. With high water output and a tankless water heater, everyone can indulge in endless hot showers or baths without any interruption. To further enhance the experience, eco-friendly earth safe soaps are provided for guests.

Kitchen:
Cook up a storm in our fully equipped kitchen, complete with an electric range and all the essential cookware. Savor a delicious home-cooked meal and start your day right with a freshly brewed cup of coffee from Little Waves Coffee, made using the Ninja Coffee Bar. Or, unwind with a relaxing cup of tea from the electric water kettle a Ninja Coffee Bar or relax with tea using the electric water kettle.

Bedrooms:
Ensure a restful night's sleep with our top-notch bedding, including two comfortable queen beds fitted with premium mattresses and high-thread count sheets. Convenient overhead reading lamps are also included for your reading pleasure.

Location
Take advantage of our home's ideal location, situated between East and West campus at Duke University and accessible to both Duke and city transit. A short 1-mile walk from downtown and a quick 10-minute stroll from 9th street means you can park your car in the driveway and explore Durham and Duke University on foot.


TV:
The house is equipped with a Roku TV. We provide Netflix and Hulu. We have all the premium Hulu channels (Live, HBO, Showtime) so this is the best service to use.

Guest access
We have super easy check-in; guests receive a unique easy to remember code that will open the door using a touchpad.

Space is yours to use as you would your own home.

Other things to note
Hot Water:
The tankless water heater may take some time to provide hot water to the apartment, just keep the water running until hot water reaches the fixture.

Noise:
There is a party wall, and the neighbors can hear you just as you can hear them. We ask that guests remain cognizant of noise, but also understanding of families with children. If you ever feel the noise is disturbing, please do let us know and we will reach out to the guests in the adjacent apartment.

listing_name: Colour Home near Downtown; Chickens, Eggs and Cats
description:
I am renting a bedroom in my renovated house near downtown Durham/NCCU. The house is old but cozy, and I’ve added chickens to the yard as my pets of choice. I also have five lovely cats, including Barly, Ora, Rascal, and more. I’m always working to make the house more welcoming and comfortable for guests.

The space
The house is a 2-story, 1947 cottage-style home with a total of 4 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms, covering about 2000 sq. ft. I am renting 4 bedrooms, and reside in a blue-painted garage ADU.

NOTE: The house is located on the busy street across from NCCU.

Both bathrooms, as well as the kitchen, have been renovated. The kitchen now features a large gas stove, double quartz sink, wood countertops, and a huge French door-style fridge with filtered water from the sink (no need for bottles). The only part that hasn’t been updated is the basement, but you’ll only need it for laundry.

You will be sharing the bathroom with me or another guest. I provide body wash, lotion, shampoo, and conditioner (usually from Whole Foods—fair trade, organic, etc.) so you don’t need to buy your own unless you have a preferred brand. All bedrooms are freshly painted, and 3 of them have queen-sized beds/mattresses. There is also a deck at the back of the house. The location is across from North Carolina Central University, making it ideal for someone visiting a student, professor, etc. It’s also convenient for Duke students, as it’s within a 5-minute drive to downtown Durham (where there’s a lively, hip atmosphere) or to Duke University. A bus that can take you there runs along the same street.

Here are the expanded HOUSE RULES for long-term tenants, which I generally prefer. I require the house to be kept very clean, whether I’m present or not. If you plan to stay longer than a week, you will be responsible for taking out the trash, recycling (let’s keep the planet clean), and making sure any online order boxes are folded and recycled as well. Every Sunday, the trash bins need to be placed by the street for Monday pickup. You must respect other guests and never leave dirty dishes in the sink. One or two plates are okay, but nothing more, as another guest might need to cook and will need a clean space.

KITCHEN, STOVE & POTS: You must clean the stove right after cooking—do not leave grease, or oil on handles, or spills. Everything must be cleaned once you finish cooking, not the next day. I provide cookware, but there are a few items I reserve for myself, such as a blue casserole by Tramontina and a yellow Great Jones casserole. Please handle the cookware gently—do not scratch or burn it. Use wood or silicone spatulas. I’ve had guests ruin pans after just three months of use, and I’m trying to avoid that now. You must cook during regular hours—no cooking at midnight or later. Keep countertops dry around the sink, and do not place hot pots or other hot items on them. Do not use them as a cutting board. The same rules apply to the dining table—it’s only for plates and eating or studying with your computer. You are also encouraged to sweep the floor. I keep cleaning supplies under the kitchen sink, and the broom is usually by the door leading to the deck. If you need help finding anything, just ask me. Please remove spoiled food from the refrigerator. The fridge is divided into four sections, so keep your items in your designated area.

BATHROOM: Keep the bathroom clean. Flush and clean the toilet after use. Do not leave shaved hair or toothpaste in the sink. Cleaning supplies are usually under the bathroom sink. Dry the floor with a mop after using the shower, and take the bathroom rug outside to dry once it gets wet.

BEDROOM: You’re also required to keep your bedroom relatively clean. This means no leftover food or beer cans for days. When you leave, you must empty your room of all your belongings, take dirty bed sheets (not the blanket but the duvet cover) to the laundry bin, and sweep/vacuum the room, including the corners and under the bed. You must also empty the refrigerator and cupboards of your food supplies. Failure to follow these rules will result in an additional $30 cleaning charge.

My cleaning standards may seem overwhelming to some, so please read them carefully before booking to make sure this is the right place for you. I don’t want anyone to feel pressured beyond their limits.

Please note that since the house is old, there may be minor issues that could bother you. Don’t hesitate to contact me immediately if you find something like an old nail or anything else that needs fixing.

Best,
Olga

Guest access
You will have access to bedroom you rent (obvious), shared bathroom, Internet, Amazon's Alexa, laundry, gas stove, french door fridge, microwave. I do not own a TV.

During your stay
I am friendly, and always glad to give advice or recommend something about Durham. If I get tough than mostly about cleaning. My main requirement is to keep house clean. The bedroom you are in and the common area. For most of the part I am often traveling so you will have plenty of space to yourself.

Other things to note
House is located on a busy road so you must be aware of the rush hours usually at 7, 8 AM, or around 4-5. Because of that, it is sometimes difficult to pull in and out of the driveway.

I also have a rooster on the property, thus if you are a light sleeper or not an early riser perhaps this is not the ideal place for you. Unless you are willing to use ear plugs.

My cat Barley is an in-and-out cat, I ask everyone to make sure she sleeps inside, just in case when I am not around to check on her.

listing_name: Rustic Historic Home~Short Walk to Downtown Durham
description:
This historic home is only minutes from downtown Durham. It sits in one of the most beautiful neighborhoods in old Durham. It’s tree lined sidewalks can take you to parks, running paths and to one of the most vibrant downtowns in North Carolina . We are happy to offer you this 4 bedroom with 2 full bathrooms home with a well stocked kitchen with most everything you made need for making those epic dinners. Our large porch is a perfect place to hang out true to the southern style of leisure.

The space
This 1930's has 4 bedroom 2 full bathrooms with showers home and has been totally renovated. It has three large bedrooms upstairs with a bathroom and shower with a tub. Another bedroom downstairs along another bathroom with a walk in shower. This beautiful old house is as close to downtown as it gets with out the noise. Our decor is clean and simple with modern features. From 100% cotton sheets to down comforters to a fully stocked kitchen and triple osmosis water filtering system is recently installed, that will please most everyone and foodies. Open floor plan exposes a comfortable mid century design living room. You can relax with access to Apple TV with Netflix and Amazon prime to enjoy. We recently added ATT fiber optics for lightining fast internet.
Dining room seats up to 8 people. But more important its about the location that will make you smile. There are too many places with in walking distance to list but here are some of my favorites. Coco Cinnamon for coffee, Full-Steam for beer, Geer street and Pizza Torro for food and Durham Yoga Co. for the soul.
My back yard backs into a park with a fun playground also free parking for two cars.
There are running trails and other activities very close.
The farmers market on Saturday is the best in the state and it's a must for visitors.
Enjoy your stay.

Guest access
Guess will have access to free parking, private bathrooms and a full kitchen.

Other things to note
Our neighbor kennels 1 large and one smaller dog in the back of their house. It gets a bit noisy at feeding times. Which is around 7 am and 6 pm. It’s also near our private parking space so they will let you know when you are near.

Dogs are allowed, but I’m sorry, we are not allowing cats at this time.

description:

listing_name: Sunny retreat, private bath
description:
Spacious master bedroom with private bath in quiet neighborhood just blocks from Duke Park & Trails, 1.5mi to heart of downtown or Duke's East Campus, 3mi to NCCU. Make my home your oasis!
Your walls are decorated with unique artwork cultivated from ArtHaus’ collective of Durham artists.
Only fully vaccinated guests, please.
No TVs in house, you share common spaces with host.

The space
This 1950's ranch includes several quaint original features - including the oven and stove top - mixed with modern upgrades, like the renovated master bathroom. Large windows and honey-colored hardwood floors make for a warm, inviting refuge during your stay in Durham. Your corner room faces southwest, infusing the space with a soft evening glow and sunset views. Get refreshed under a rain-fall showerhead with 3 settings in a custom extra-large shower!

Basement is home to the washer/dryer, but also a mini gym with spin bike, Nordic Track, kettle bells and power tower. So you've got a home gym if you decide not to take advantage of the copious trails or classes nearby!

You’ll notice unique and beautiful art on the walls, courtesy of ArtHaus. Beginning June 15, my home gallery will feature the work of Durham artists DL Anderson [dlandersondotcom] and Reid Rosemond. All pieces you see during your stay are available to purchase from the artist.

Please note: you are NOT renting my whole home, but the master bedroom with private bath, and shared access to kitchen/dining and yard. I do still live here ;-).

Guest access
You will have full access to the kitchen, front porch and back yard(including gas grill).

There will be locally roasted coffee and tea available for you to make at any time.
Please just clean up after yourself!

You're welcome to use the washer and clothes lines/drying racks (detergent provided) in the basement.

Your room has a locking door, and locked or not I respect my guests privacy. I will not enter the room during your reservation unless there's an emergency. I expect the same respect from my guests.

During your stay

listing_name: Modern, Quality 4BR Near Downtown
description:
Discover your home away from home in this spacious 4-bedroom house in Durham! Ideal for families and groups, this single-level home boasts a modern design, a fully equipped kitchen, and a private backyard. Enjoy the convenience of a dedicated workspace and the tranquility of a maple lined winding driveway, all while being just minutes from downtown Durham and Duke University. Experience the perfect blend of comfort and accessibility.

The space
Step into a light-filled, modern home with an elegant, streamlined style. The open-plan living room is the perfect central gathering space, flowing into a fully equipped kitchen with striking granite counters and stainless steel appliances. Share meals at the beautiful hardwood locally harvested dining table.

Our home comfortably accommodates your group with four thoughtfully designed bedrooms:
•King Bedroom: An en-suite retreat with French doors, a granite double vanity, and a luxurious spa-like river rock shower.
•Second Primary Bedroom: A family-friendly suite with a full tub and a granite vanity.
•Two Queen Bedrooms: Two additional rooms with queen beds; one room with a dedicated desk, and the other a reading chair. Rooms share a hall bathroom featuring riverstone flooring in the shower, a glass enclosure, and a granite counter.

Step outside to relax on the vaulted front porch with its inviting up-lighting, or have fun with yard games in the private, fenced-in backyard. For your convenience, the home includes an in-unit washer and dryer.

Guest access
You will have exclusive and private access to the entire home. This includes all four bedrooms, bathrooms, the kitchen, living areas, the front porch, and the fenced-in backyard.

Enjoy seamless self-check-in with a keypad on the front door, allowing you to arrive at your convenience any time after check-in. Free parking is available on the premises for your vehicles.

•Live TV: We have 'cut the cord' on traditional cable. For live TV, sports, and on-demand shows, we provide access to YouTubeTV.

listing_name: Cozy Private Tiny Home Walk to Downtown Durham NC
description:
The perfect spot for a cozy getaway right next to downtown. This tiny gem is nestled in a private back yard just a short walk to downtown and Durham's entertainment district where you will find breweries, restaurants and cafès. Its 200 sqft include a lofted sleeping area, a main living area equipped with a daybed & small table, a kitchenette with induction burner, and a private bathroom with a modern composting toilet & shower.

*If booking on day of, stay check-in may start at 8pm*

The space
Our tiny house is built out of blue pine which gives the interior a warm glow in the morning light. The space is small and cozy but perfect for a single person or a couple who is used to sharing.

The lofted queen bed sleeps two and guests may request to have the downstairs daybed equipped for the night if they find they need more space or prefer not to share. The daybed is very comfortable but somewhat skinny - smaller people will be the most comfortable sleeping there.

The kitchenette has a sink and an induction burner and contains all that's needed to prepare coffee or tea (provided), or a simple meal. A mini fridge is available for food storage.

A small fold-out table by the picture window can be used for eating or working. Please note that this is not an office desk with ergonomic seating. It's a very small work surface and the seating is a storage ottoman (but you can bring in a patio char if you prefer). The wording listed under our amenities list was added by Airbnb based on user feedback and unfortunately we don't have access to it to change so it better reflects the properties of our unique listing.

The bathroom features a full sized shower and a Nature's Head Composting Toilet. While this is nothing like the composting toilet from your days at camp, it does function differently than a standard toilet. Specifically, guests of all anatomies are asked to sit to ensure proper performance. Please make sure to specify that you've read this part of the listing when requesting to book. We want all our guests to know what to expect before they come so they can have a positive experience.

If you aren't sure if this is for you you may want to check out this youtube video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=24LimUrb4LM
Video not showing? Search for “Nature Head Composting Toilet”

We love our tiny house and hope you will too.

Guest access
The tiny house is located in a quiet private backyard. Sometimes let the back part of the yard grow tall to promote wildflowers that keep bees happy and attract other pollinators. You are welcome to serve yourself to any herbs or veggies that might be growing in the garden boxes. The yard is a work in progress and rustic- on the weekends, you might see us weeding, mulching, or installing rain water barrels.

Other things to note
If this is your first Airbnb stay, please make sure to verify your Government ID with Airbnb before requesting to book.

We ask that guests only use the biodegradable soaps/shampoos/cleaning products provided. Our tiny home is eco-friendly and can't handle other types of soaps.

Please keep in mind that this listing is for a tiny home - it is perfect for the right kind of traveler but it may not fit all the needs of those seeking a high-end or typical hotel experience or those who can't comfortably climb up and down a ladder to access the bed. If you are seeking clean, warm, and homey, then we know you'll appreciate the simple joys this tiny house has to offer such as curling up with a book while being serenaded by rain on the tin roof.

listing_name: Cozy Cottage
description:
Light and cozy, a great place to unwind. Roughly a half mile to Watts Grocery, Monuts, Joe Van Gogh Coffee and Duke's East Campus is just down the road. It's also a stone's throw from the School of Science and Math. Enjoy a walk on the Ellerbee Creek Trail or hop in an lyft/uber for a quick 5-10 minute ride to downtown to enjoy the evening night life! 5-8 min from Duke Hospital, Sarah P. Duke Gardens and more.

Or stay home and enjoy relaxing on the back porch or in the garden area!

The space
Cozy post-war cottage in a friendly neighborhood. Lots of dog walkers, runners, and lots of old trees hang over the streets. Beautiful hardwood floors inside, nice natural light . Enjoy the back porch and patio table to eat outside overlooking an incredibly huge sycamore out back amidst a garden or sit inside the breakfast nook while enjoying your morning coffee.

Feel like taking a walk? We are a 1/2 mile from lots of little spots to eat on/near Broad St. (Monuts, Joe Van Gogh coffee, Oval Park Grill), or stroll over to Ninth Street to find a ton more. Or take a slightly longer walk to Duke's east campus to use their path around the school. The School of Science and Math is within walking distance. Enjoy the parks at Oval Park and the Ellerbee Creek trail - all within a stones throw. Golfer? We are close to that too!

Other things to note
I have allotted a budget for the month in terms of utilities and if you or your guests go over this amount, I will ask you to please cover the difference. Happy to discuss the amount, but generally if it's over $100 per power, water, gas, etc..

listing_name: The Stable: a peace-filled, creative retreat
description:
Welcome to The Stable - a serene solo respite, artist’s retreat or unique getaway for 1 to 6 registered guests. Close to I-85 & I-40, Duke, NCCU and the downtown Durham scene. Quaint & artsy Hillsborough, funky & fun Chapel Hill/UNC and Carrboro are a short drive. Eno River State Park, hiking & biking trails, and the Mountains to Sea Trail are 2.5 miles away. Full of unexpected details, including an outside labyrinth, The Stable is a comfortable sanctuary for all who want to settle in and smile.

The space
Upon Arrival: Flat, gravel driveway with motion light and ample parking. Walkway with four steps to front door with keyless entry.

Living/Dining Room: Spacious and open with vaulted ceiling, rafters and ceiling fan. Well-lit, tall windows, double doors that open to screened porch. Comfortable seating area and dining table for six. 12 built-in cubbies for stashing your things. Lots of coat hooks. Curtains and shutters if you would like extra privacy. Note: The Stable does not have a TV, on purpose. ;-)

Kitchen: Fully equipped, ready for cooking, baking, meal prep, dining or the proverbial hang out. Smooth, cooktop range, dishwasher, microwave, full-sized fridge with ice maker, hardwood countertops/breakfast bar, 12-cup coffee maker (with filters), Keurig (with a few starter K-cups), French press, electric kettle, toaster, blender and can opener. We've been told that we thought of everything!

Office Nook: Well-lit space adjacent to kitchen with desktop area for laptops/tablets. Wi-Fi, outlets, device charger, basic office supplies, radio, two stools and a little stained-glass sun ornament from Ocracoke Island.

Bathroom: Large, galvanized metal, walk-in shower with grab bar (no tub), soap, shampoo, conditioner, wash cloths, hand towels, bath towels, hooks, towel racks and metal bins for your belongings and a hairdryer. Oh, and a crank out window, too. This bathroom neatly fits it all.

Ground floor Bedroom: Queen size bed featuring Sealy Posturepedic plush Euro pillow top mattress for a perfect night's sleep. Wake up to the sun shining through the trees. There are night stands with wall lights on both sides of the bed with lap trays (think breakfast in bed!) and outlets close by. The closet has hangers, ironing board/iron, extra pillows, blanket, card table and extra chairs. A dresser, two suitcase stands, a ceiling fan and room-darkening curtains all make for a super comfortable bedroom experience. ZZZZZ!

The Loft: Ascend the standard staircase to a spacious, full height, sleeping loft with dutch (Mr. Ed) door. Four twin beds with individual nightstands, hooks, lamps, outlet, and shelves create a little privacy for each guest. Dresser with 6 drawers, a ceiling fan, mirror and 2 windows all make for a fun, comfortable, up and away space. No need to duck down in this loft!

Screened Porch: Large, open gathering space with a peaceful wooded view and a custom-made, wooden rowboat filled with pillows for reading and napping. A long table, chairs, ceiling fan and utility sink. Step outside the porch and enter the 5-circuit labyrinth for a meditative stroll. Picnic table in back yard and a fire pit (use only upon request, please)

Guest access
The entire space, inside and out, is yours to enjoy. No more than 6, REGISTERED guests at anytime during your stay. Not suitable for infants or children under 12. There are a few shelves and bins marked \owner's only\". Please respect this.

Other things to note
Claudia is your main contact and is quick to answer your message via the Airbnb app and to provide any assistance to make your stay worry-free and comfortable.

We do welcome weekly or monthly stays. If our calendar appears to be booked

The space
Modern, bright and beautiful. This space has been completely renovated, it has tall ceilings, big windows and beautiful farmhouse finishes, brand new kitchen and island. Glass shower. Washer and Dryer at your disposal. Over 1000 SF for a 2 bedroom.

This space is the right side of a duplex separated by a center wall and accessed via a private entrance. The entire space is yours and no interior spaces are shared.

Once inside, the property opens to a living room with a couch and two chairs, coffee table and smart tv where you can access your streaming services.

The Trifecta on Morgan Street is a charming 2 bedroom, 2 bath rental in the historical Warehouse District of Downtown Durham that includes a fully stocked kitchen, comfortably furnished rooms, and free parking on-site. It's within walking distance to the convention center and Downtown Durham's best restaurants, pubs, and entertainment.

You'll love the 18th century architectural roots of the space which features brick, timber, high ceilings, wood floors & an open floor plan. It'll be your home away from home while you explore and experience everything Durham has to offer. We love to give recommendations to our guests so never hesitate to ask us for things to do, places to eat, or sights to see.

What you'll really like about staying at our space:

• Cleanliness. Our space is professionally cleaned before each and every reservation, while many other listings in the city are not. You'll really appreciate staying at a clean and well taken care of space.

• Communication. We have a 100% response rate for a reason. We respond to each and every guest message within just a few minutes, before and during your stay.

• Arrival. We have a convenient 24/7 self-check-in system with free on-site parking that lets our guests have flexible check-in times, no matter how late it is at night.

• Safety. Our townhome is located in an apartment community that has security cameras, is well-lit, and has an enclosed garage for private parking. We also have an outside camera above the hallway entrance that we use to help us conveniently check-in guests, and as an added security benefit for our guests. These cameras do have audio recording that we are able to review history of if needed. There are no cameras or audio recording devices inside.

• Location. The location we're in is a walker's paradise since most places you'll want to visit won't require a car. Morgan Street is as flat as a pancake, has ample sidewalk space, and is very bikeable. Our home is just a 5-minute walk away from the Convention Center. Walk Score is 84, Transit Score 65 and Bike Score 80.

Everything you need to know about our home:

• two spacious bedrooms each with a queen-sized bed
• Apple TV (must use your own personal subscription for apps) and tv antenna in both living room and one of the bedrooms
• super fast wifi, perfect for apple tv streaming
• two full bathrooms (one with tub, one with standing shower)
• walk-in closet that'll hold all your suitcases, travel bags, and clothing
• paid washer and dryer down the hallway (not in our unit)
• black-out curtains over the large windows
• fully stocked kitchen with the essential cookware, silverware, and dinnerware you'll need to dine-in
• dining table
• spacious living room that includes a queen-sized pull out sofa
• laptop friendly workspace

Please take a look at the photos in the listing to see our home and feel free to message us with any questions you may have regarding our rental. We typically respond in a few minutes.

*Important Note: This rental is located in a historic tobacco warehouse that has been converted into residential apartments but still maintains older warehouse fixtures. The walls for the bedrooms do not reach the 22 foot ceiling, this can be seen in the pictures. Also, since this is an industrial setting, some noise does carry from surrounding neighbors. The parking is not on the ground level of the building, but rather 1 block away. Let us know if you have any further questions about these notes!

*Important Note. We require a photo of our guests' driver's license/state id during their stay. Please do not inquire or instant book if you cannot provide a photo of your driver's license/state id. There are OUTDOOR surveillance cameras

listing_name: Cozy One Level Ranch- 5 mins to DT & DPAC
description:
Welcome to our 3BR ranch home in Durham — a comfortable retreat for families, medical travelers, corporate guests, and anyone looking for a true home-away-from-home experience in the Bull City.

Conveniently situated for quick access to everything Durham has to offer — without the noise and parking headaches of being downtown:

5 minutes to Downtown Durham, Durham Bulls Park & DPAC
10 minutes to Duke University & Duke Hospital
15 minutes to Research Triangle Park (RTP)
20 minutes to RDU Airport

The space
Add our listing to your wishlist by clicking the ♥ in upper-right hand corner

This fully furnished 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom home features hardwood floors throughout, tiled bathrooms, and a bright open-concept living and dining area. The kitchen is fully equipped with stainless steel appliances including a dishwasher, microwave, and full-size stove and refrigerator — everything you need to cook and settle in.

All three bedrooms are furnished with queen-size beds. The master bedroom includes a dresser and smart TV. The washer and dryer are on-site for your use, and we provide complimentary coffee, tea, and WiFi throughout your stay.

Getting Around:
A car or rideshare is your best bet from this location, and both are easy. Uber and Lyft are consistently available, and parking at all major Durham destinations is straightforward. The private driveway accommodates your vehicle for the duration of your stay.

Guest access
The entire home and yard are yours for the duration of your stay. Check-in is fully self-guided using a smart keypad with your own unique access code — no key exchanges, no coordination needed. You'll have complete privacy from arrival to checkout.

Other things to note
We've designed this space to feel like a real home, not a hotel room. We stock the basics, maintain the property carefully, and respond quickly if anything comes up. We simply ask that you treat the space with the same respect you'd want for your own home.

listing_name: CHARM & Convenience!! 3Br Cottage-Style Ranch
description:
This home is located in the more DESIRABLE and 'not as congested' low crime area of Durham near Chapel Hill, on a half acre lot in the rural buffer zone! You're really convenient to Duke Hospital/Duke campus, and also just a very short drive to Chapel Hill! The backyard is BIG and completely fenced in for family games or just enjoying the birds, or your dog's exercise! Duke Forest trails and New Hope Creek are VERY nearby for a wonderful hike during your stay.

The space
This house offers a cozy open floor-plan from the kitchen to the living room/dining area that's ideal for families or friends to spend time together and socialize, while at the same time there are separate areas that can be closed off for privacy on either side of the house. One side of the house has 2 of the bedrooms along with a full bathroom with tub/shower combination. The other side of the house has the washer/dryer, another full bathroom (with shower) and 3rd bedroom! Enjoy time in the backyard for an additional peaceful retreat!
Please note: This is not a 'fancy' house, there are a few water stains on the laminate flooring in a couple places and the furniture is not modern or new. However, although not a newly remodeled house, it's a very comfortable well-lived in older home to enjoy! We have always taken cleaning very seriously , and during these times when it's essential for all of us to have great confidence in the cleanliness of our spaces, we want you to know that in between guest groups ALL surfaces, knobs, and handles are thoroughly cleaned with professional cleaners that kill viruses and bacteria.

Other things to note
There's 3 ROKU TV's in the bedrooms and in the living room connected to fast Spectrum Internet, and you can watch Sling TV which contains live programming and news shows!
We welcome your dog and we charge a pet fee of $9 per night/per dog. Compared to other rentals this is a bargain, some places charge a large flat fee regardless of the length of your stay!

listing_name: Cozy Poolside Studio Apartment
description:
Cozy efficiency apartment over looking swimming pool in renovated 1913 home in Historic Trinity Park

* Within a few blocks of theaters, restaurants, Duke campus & much more!

* High ceilings, lots of natural light, roomy closet

* Small kitchen, coffee included

* Access to pool (typically June-Oct), gas grill, washer/dryer & more

* Hosts are usually close by and happy to accommodate requests quickly (and enjoy meeting people from near and far)

* Pets welcome

The space
* There is street parking and a driveway next to the house for unloading and loading

* Access to apartment is via door in fence (with combo padlock) at end of driveway on left side of house

* Enter apt via French doors with codes on dead bolt, no key necessary

* We have friendly dogs (see photo) who get along with people and other pets (mostly indoors, but occasionally by the pool)

* PLEASE NOTE: The house next door (another 100-year-old historic home) is in its final phases of being restored. This includes redoing the driveway. Therefore the path to the pool gate is currently on hard packed gravel (not concrete). This should be resolved by the end of May 2026.

ALSO, the commercial district is a block away from this apartment. That includes bars frequented by local students - and means that on the weekends street traffic, music and bar noise can be loud into the early hours. We do provide a noise machine.

This is one of four apartments in the house, so there may be other guests in or around the pool area, and you may hear footsteps or occasional noises from above (but not after quiet hours, please).

The backyard is fully fenced and includes a pool - all shared between the 4 apartments in the house. The pool (and zipline!) is available seasonally - typically June-October, as pool is not heated - at your own risk. Please check in with us for actual open dates. NOTE: as of July 2022 - pool is repaired and open!

And finally, the house and pool were built a long time ago, so they have plenty of \charm\" (but most guests don't mind ;-).

Guest access
You'll have the whole apartment to yourself

description:

The space

listing_name: Dollar Avenue Treehouse near Duke
description:
Dollar Avenue Treehouse is situated on a quiet three-block long street lined with signature Durham oak trees in the historic Trinity Park neighborhood. Steps away from Duke’s East Campus, this charming, newly renovated one-bedroom apartment is a pleasant stroll or short bike, scooter, or Lyft/Uber ride from downtown, Brightleaf Square, 9th Street, Old North Durham, DPAC, Museum of Life and Science (via Ellerbe Creek Trail), parks, and numerous restaurants and bars.

The space
A newly remodeled garage apartment with its own private access and sweet sitting area nestled in native trees, plant and flowers.

Great spot for conference goers, visiting academics, traveling families or small groups of friends. The space is comfortable, conveniently situated, and fun!

Entire space available for guests.

The apartment has one bed, a pull-out couch, one bathroom, and a kitchenette (mini-fridge, microwave, toaster oven, coffee & tea service). Light streams through trees from skylights and windows. Plenty of on street parking.

There’s wifi, TV, and a Bluetooth speaker for your enjoyment. There are three grocery stores within walking or biking/scooter distance as are numerous restaurants, including Juju, Rose’s, Goorsha, Monuts, Elmo’s (family friendly) Cocoa Cinnamon, Parker and Otis.

There is a park (Trinity Park) and an elementary school (George Watts), both with spacious playgrounds and shaded areas with trees three blocks away. We also highly recommend the indoor/outdoor Museum of Life and Science and Duke Park if you want to thrill the little ones. If you have slightly older ones (or are a kid at heart yourself), there is a skate park at Durham Central Park (great farmer’s market there on Saturdays), also walkable. If you need kid or baby-gear let us know! We have two of our own so may be able to hook you up!

Keypad and code make for easy access for guests. Host are available by phone/text for any problems that arise, to give suggestions, or render any help needed; we are usually onsite in the main house.

Check-in is anytime after 3PM
Check-out is at 11am
No parties.
The unit is in a residential neighborhood. Please be respectful with your noise. Quiet hours after 10pm.

Guest access
We'll provide you with a Smartlock code for the front door after you book your stay. You can check-in anytime after 3PM, and come and go as you please.

Other things to note
There is a Bluetooth speaker for music.

listing_name: Historic Durham Mill House | Cozy 2BR Retreat
description:
Stay in a thoughtfully restored historic mill house in Durham’s creative Golden Belt district. This bright 2BR/2BA home blends original character with modern comfort, making it an ideal home base for visiting Duke, downtown Durham, or the Triangle.
Highlights:
• Newly fenced backyard perfect for dogs
• Renovated kitchen + cooking essentials
• Comfortable beds + thoughtful touches
• Workspace, fast WiFi & Smart TV
• 2 full bathrooms
• Easy access to Duke, downtown & RTP

The space
This thoughtfully restored historic mill house combines Durham character with the comfort and convenience guests want for short visits, longer stays, work trips, and weekends away.

Inside you’ll find:
• 2 private bedrooms and 2 full bathrooms
• Comfortable beds and cozy living spaces
• Renovated kitchen stocked for cooking at home
• Smart TV with streaming access
• Dedicated workspace + fast WiFi
• Washer/dryer for longer stays
• Pet-friendly accommodations
• Newly fenced backyard with direct access from the home

The fully fenced backyard is especially convenient for guests traveling with dogs, offering a secure outdoor space directly off the back entrance. While outdoor furnishings are still minimal, there’s plenty of room to relax, play, or enjoy fresh air during your stay.

The split-bedroom layout offers added privacy for couples, friends, families, or longer-term guests.

Hosted by Delighted Properties, a local Durham hospitality company, the home includes thoughtful touches throughout — from local recommendations to carefully selected amenities designed to help you settle in comfortably.

Located in Durham’s historic Golden Belt area, the home offers easy access to downtown Durham, Duke, NCCU, RTP, breweries, restaurants, and major highways.

Please note:
This is a historic urban home near an active roadway, so guests sensitive to city sounds may prefer to bring earplugs for sleeping. We provide a sound machine in each bedroom.

Guest access
Guests will have full private access to the home and property, including free street parking for up to 2 vehicles.

Enjoy access to the fenced backyard and front porch during your stay — ideal for morning coffee, relaxing after a day in the city, or spending time with your pets.

Other things to note
A few helpful notes before booking:

• Quiet hours begin at 10 PM out of respect for neighbors
• No parties or events permitted
• Smoking or vaping is not allowed anywhere on the property
• Up to 2 well-behaved dogs are welcome
• Pets must be supervised while outdoors
• Please note the home is not specifically child-proofed
• Washer/dryer units are compact and best suited for smaller loads
• Reservations longer than 29 nights may require additional fees depending on the length and details of the stay. If applicable, these will be discussed prior to confirmation.
• For the safety of our guests, neighbors, and properties, we use a secure third-party screening provider, Safely, as part of the reservation process. By confirming the reservation, guests agree to this screening. Guests may receive a follow-up email to verify identity and confirm reservation details. Please check your spam folder if you do not see this message.
• Airbnb Services or Experiences hosted by third parties are not permitted at the property without prior written approval. Unauthorized events, services, productions, or commercial activities may result in immediate cancellation of the reservation without refund.

We appreciate guests who treat the home with care and help us maintain a comfortable experience for everyone who stays here.

listing_name: 1BR Bungalow Near Duke Hospital | Quiet & Private
description:
This quaint and quiet Walltown Bungalow is located in Durham NC within walking distance to Duke University’s East Campus, North Carolina School of Science & Mathematics, 9th street restaurants, and several grocery stores. Private entrance, remodeled kitchen, in unit washer/dryer + all of the conveniences of home. Easy access to I-85 and 147.

The space
There is a private entrance on the back side of the house. A fully stocked kitchen with all new appliances, a bathroom with a tub. The living room has a trundle that can be used as a bed. Both the bed and the trundle are very comfortable! There is also a washer & dryer in the unit.

Guest access
Walk down the driveway, and through the gate to get to the private entrance, where you're welcome to set and enjoy a cup of coffee on the private porch in the morning. You can also use the gas grill in the back yard, there's a table and chairs on the deck. If you're lucky enough to book this space over the 4th of July weekend, the church behind the house put on an unbelievable fireworks display.

Other things to note
The bungalow is completely private. The only shared space is the backyard. There is plenty of street parking and 99.99% of the time you'll be able to park directly in front of the house.

listing_name: Modern Tiny House Near Duke & Downtown
description:
You’ll feel like you’re getting away from it all in this modern, private tiny house in the trees, even though you’re just minutes from Duke, downtown Durham, shopping, and restaurants. It’s the perfect balance of peaceful retreat and city convenience.

The home includes all the essentials — full kitchen, laundry, A/C, and high-speed internet — but many guests find themselves spending most of their time relaxing on the screened-in porch, listening to birds, and recharging.

The space
Sleep & Comfort:

You can’t put a price on good sleep. We’ve outfitted the tiny house with a high-density foam mattress and luxury hotel-quality sheets to ensure a comfortable, restful night.

Kitchen & Amenities:

-The kitchen is stocked with staples, including quality coffee and tea, so you can settle in right away.

-Please note: there is no television. The space is intentionally designed for unplugging, slowing down, and connecting with nature or with your companion.

Guest access
The tiny home is a private space, all yours.

Please note that it has quite a woodsy feel. We're still in the center of the city in a quiet neighborhood. :)

Other things to note
Sustainability & Values:

We believe everyone should feel welcome here. Our home is intentionally inclusive and supportive of diversity.

We also partner with One Tree Planted— planting one tree for every guest stay.
🌱 Thanks to our guests, 1,870 trees have been planted to date.

listing_name: Stylish living close to RTP, UNC, & Duke!
description:
Relax or work in a quiet neighborhood that is close to restaurants, shopping, and colleges in our stylish townhouse! Nestled in a quiet and safe community, you would never know your only mins. away from all the action; 9 to RTP, 15 to UNC, 20 to Duke, and 16 to RDU airport.


*Please note- we are requiring a minimum of 4 night stay over Christmas.

The space
The home is styled with vintage and modern pieces that will make you feel welcome and comfortable. We offer a fully equipped kitchen, laundry room, back porch, fenced in yard, free parking in front of the home, and lots of closet space. Perfect for six people but can accommodate eight if you are willing to use the pull out couch. *Please note there is 1 step down from the parking to the walkway which may not be ADA accessible to some

Master Bedroom: Furnished with a Calvin Klein queen size four poster bed, a Tuft and Needle mattress, and comfortable bedding; you'll find it's pure comfort! There is an ensuite bath.

Second Bedroom: It is furnished with a comfortable pillow top mattress and high quality bedding and a large closet with plenty of space.

Third Bedroom: The third bedroom is towards the front of the house and has a large sunny window. There are two twin beds with twin xl Thomasville Fusion memory foam mattresses. One of the beds is a beautiful daybed that provides extra lounge space or an additional bed for sleeping. This room also has two large closets to hang your clothes.

*We have an infant crib that can be easily assembled with plenty of space in the 3rd room or Master along with a toddler mattress that can be placed on the floor for our littlest travelers. We also have a booster seat and some toys for our under 2 guests in the hall closet. While we welcome young children in our home *NOTE* it is not baby proofed and there are many dangers for young explorers. You will be responsible for ensuring they play safely in the home.

The living room offers a pull out sofa with space to fit up to two guests. The 49 inch UHD 4K Smart TV in the living room is ready to stream Netflix and broadcasts local channels. You can also access Hulu, Amazon, Vevo, ... etc. on your own personal subscriptions. And if you're here for business or have to catch up on work, there is a small workspace. Find games, toys and charging cables in the chest of drawers in this space left for your use.

A fully equipped kitchen makes cooking a breeze for those nights when you'd rather stay in. The kitchen also includes a cozy eat-in nook for additional seating apart from the dinning room.

We’ve recently add a gas fire pit and Adirondack chairs for a comfortable place to relax outdoors.
Complimentary coffee will be available with your choice of french press or Cuisinart coffee/tea maker as well as an assortment of delicious teas, cream and sugar.
The home has a laundry room with washer and dryer as well as an iron and ironing board available during your stay.

Other things to note
Updated policy- Nothing that resembles a party allowed. Absolutely no smoking allowed. If the home has any residual smell of smoke (cigarettes, marijuana, Juul, ect) there will be an automatic $250 fee. No guests other than those listed in the reservation are allowed to stay in the home. Breaking any of these rules, yet not limited to these, will result in immediate removal.

While we understand accidents happen, please alert us immediately of any stains you find on linens upon your arrival as stained item found after your stay may result in an extra charge. We regret needing to implement this but far too many guests are careless with our home and don’t leave it in respectful conditions.

By confirming your reservation with us, you are agreeing to the policies above.

Guests booking over Christmas night must have a 4 night minimum stay.

listing_name: Luxurious, Clean Private Room near Triangle & Duke
description:
Welcome to your oasis on Brook Lane, Durham 27712!

Our home is a custom-built contemporary with all the mid-century touches.

Your room is designed specifically for a relaxing vacation. You'll feel at home the second you enter with comfortable, clean furnishings and convenient amenities.

We make check-in easy with contactless instructions right in the app.

Read through the listing and ask any questions to ensure the best possible fit for your travels.

The space
The Space
​This bright upper-level suite features a king-size bed and a private attached bathroom with an incredible rainfall shower. Six upper-story windows flood the room with natural light, making it an ideal choice for remote work or long stays. We provide HBO and YouTube TV on the Roku, along with a fan, white noise machine, and air purifier for your comfort.

​Natural Light Note
​Please note that the upper windows do not have coverings. You will awaken each morning to beautiful, natural light streaming into the room. If you prefer a dark room for sleeping in, you may wish to bring a sleep mask or consider one of our other rooms.

​Kitchenette
​The shared common area includes a fridge, microwave, small toaster oven, and coffee maker. While there is no stovetop or oven, it is perfect for warming up prepared meals or enjoying a quick morning coffee.

1970s Character & Sound
As a preserved vintage home, sound travels between floors. Two guest rooms are located directly below this room.

​Parking
​Your stay includes one reserved driveway parking spot. Local regulations do not permit street parking, so please ensure you arrive in a single vehicle.

Guest access
Please use Brook Lane in Durham 27712 for estimated directions and distances.

Upon booking we provide a code for you to enter the house and your private room. This code is specific to you to ensure safety and security.

During your stay
This being a shared house, you may see other guests in the common areas.

Ruth and Zack do live on site and would be happy to meet you to just say hi or to recommend a great restaurant nearby.

Other things to note
​Location & Arrival
Please map our location (Brook Lane, Durham 27712) prior to booking to ensure the commute works for your plans.

​Climate Control
To ensure the comfort of all guests in this shared vintage home, the heating and AC system is centrally controlled across all three levels. Individual room temperatures cannot be adjusted, and the thermostat remains at a fixed setting.

​Water & Amenities
Our tap water is filtered and completely safe for drinking. Please note that only the amenities specifically listed are provided; if you have questions about something not mentioned, feel free to ask before you book.

The space
The Trifecta on Morgan Street is a charming 2 bedroom, 1.5 bath rental in the historical Warehouse District of Downtown Durham that includes a fully stocked kitchen, comfortably furnished rooms, and free parking on-site. It's within walking distance to the convention center and Downtown Durham's best restaurants, pubs, and entertainment.

You'll love the 18th century architectural roots of the space which features brick, timber, high ceilings, wood floors & an open floor plan. It'll be your home away from home while you explore and experience everything Durham has to offer. We love to give recommendations to our guests so never hesitate to ask us for things to do, places to eat, or sights to see.

What you'll really like about staying at our space:

• Cleanliness. Our space is professionally cleaned before each and every reservation, while many other listings in the city are not. You'll really appreciate staying at a clean and well taken care of space.

• Communication. We have a 100% response rate for a reason. We respond to each and every guest message within just a few minutes, before and during your stay.

• Arrival. We have a convenient 24/7 self-check-in system with free on-site parking that lets our guests have flexible check-in times, no matter how late it is at night.

• Safety. Our townhome is located in an apartment community that has security cameras, is well-lit, and has an enclosed garage for private parking. We also have an outside camera above the hallway entrance that we use to help us conveniently check-in guests, and as an added security benefit for our guests. These cameras do have audio recording that we are able to review history of if needed. There are no cameras or audio recording devices inside.

• Location. The location we're in is a walker's paradise since most places you'll want to visit won't require a car. Morgan Street is as flat as a pancake, has ample sidewalk space, and is very bikeable. Our home is just a 5-minute walk away from the Convention Center. Walk Score is 84, Transit Score 65 and Bike Score 80.

Everything you need to know about our home:

• two spacious bedrooms each with a queen-sized bed
• Apple TV (must use your own personal subscription for apps) and tv antenna in living room
• super fast wifi, perfect for apple tv streaming
• 1.5 bathrooms (one full bath, one half bath)
• walk-in closet that'll hold all your suitcases, travel bags, and clothing
• paid washer/dryer on the 2nd floor the hallway (not in our unit, shared with other residents)
• black-out curtains over the large windows
• fully stocked kitchen with the essential cookware, silverware, and dinnerware you'll need to dine-in
• dining table
• spacious living room that includes a queen-sized pull out sofa
• laptop friendly workspace

Please take a look at the photos in the listing to see our home and feel free to message us with any questions you may have regarding our rental. We typically respond in a few minutes.

*Important Note: This rental is located in a historic tobacco warehouse that has been converted into residential apartments but still maintains older warehouse fixtures. The walls for the bedrooms do not reach the 22 foot ceiling, this can be seen in the pictures. Also, since this is an industrial setting, some noise does carry from surrounding neighbors. The parking is not on the ground level of the building, but rather 1 block away. Let us know if you have any further questions about these notes!

*Important Note. We require a photo of our guests' driver's license/state id during their stay. Please do not inquire or instant book if you cannot provide a photo of your driver's license/state id. More information regarding this standard procedure can be found in the \Other things to note\" section.
